title: Add takt operator skill deployed by takt init
description:
Create a Claude Code skill at .claude/skills/takt/ that teaches the operator's assistant how to manage a takt-orchestrated project. This skill should be bundled in src/agent_takt/_data/claude_skills/takt/ and deployed by takt init alongside the existing skill-spec-management skill.

The skill should cover:
- What beads are and their lifecycle (open → ready → in_progress → done | blocked | handed_off)
- All key CLI commands: takt summary, takt bead list/show/create/delete, takt plan, takt run, takt merge, takt retry, takt tui
- Planning workflow: draft spec → takt plan --write → set status planned
- Scheduler workflow: takt --runner claude run --once --max-workers N
- Merge workflow: takt merge <bead_id>, what to do when merge-conflict beads are created
- Moving a spec to done
- Common mistakes to avoid

The skill is for the operator's Claude Code assistant (lives in .claude/skills/), NOT for worker agents (.agents/skills/). Model it after the existing skill-spec-management skill structure (SKILL.md with frontmatter + content).

Files to create:
- src/agent_takt/_data/claude_skills/takt/SKILL.md

Files to modify:
- src/agent_takt/onboarding.py — deploy claude_skills/takt/ alongside the existing claude_skills entries

handoff_summary.completed: Created `src/agent_takt/_data/claude_skills/takt/SKILL.md` with full operator guidance for managing a takt-orchestrated project.
handoff_summary.risks: None. The new skill is purely additive — a new directory under an already-recursively-deployed path.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — new file in a new directory; no overlap with other beads.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: `onboarding.py` was not modified. The `install_claude_skills()` function already performs a recursive `rglob("*")` copy of the entire `_data/claude_skills/` directory into `.claude/skills/`, so any new subdirectory placed there (including `takt/`) is automatically deployed without further changes. Modifying `onboarding.py` to explicitly handle `takt/` would have been redundant and inconsistent with how `memory/` and `skill-spec-management/` are already handled. The SKILL.md content mirrors the authoritative operator guidance in CLAUDE.md but is structured as a skill (with YAML frontmatter) for consumption by the operator's Claude Code assistant.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify: (1) `install_claude_skills()` deploys `takt/SKILL.md` to `.claude/skills/takt/SKILL.md` in a fresh `scaffold_project()` call; (2) the SKILL.md has valid YAML frontmatter parseable by the skill loader; (3) `takt init` on a clean project creates the file at the expected path.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: The skill content references `uv run takt` commands and `spec.py` workflows; it will need updating if CLI commands change in future beads. The `takt merge` section mentions "currently: `uv run python -m unittest discover`" in the existing skill-spec-management skill — the new takt skill intentionally omits that version-specific detail in favour of the generic description.

conflict_risks: None — new file in a new directory; no overlap with other beads.

execution_history.summary: Created `src/agent_takt/_data/claude_skills/takt/SKILL.md` — a comprehensive operator skill covering bead lifecycle, all key CLI commands, planning/scheduler/merge workflows, moving specs to done, and common mistakes. No changes to `onboarding.py` were required: `install_claude_skills()` already does a recursive copy of the entire `_data/claude_skills/` directory, so the new `takt/SKILL.md` is automatically deployed by `takt init`.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
